title: pedilonum gnomus
side_effects: Pedilonum gnomus can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea, dizziness, and headaches, particularly when ingested in large quantities. In some cases, prolonged exposure to the plant's toxins can lead to liver damage, skin irritation, and allergic reactions. Overuse or incorrect application of pedilonum gnomus extracts can also result in digestive issues, such as diarrhea, stomach cramps, and vomiting. Additionally, individuals with pre-existing medical conditions may experience worsened symptoms or interactions with other medications when using pedilonum gnomus improperly.
